evals.name: grok-x-and-web-sources
evals.prompt: Extract a Grok paste with user prompts, assistant controls, X post cards, web citations, and tool-progress blocks.

expectations.text: Response uses assistant controls and tool-progress boundaries as role evidence.
Response catalogs X posts and web citations as source traces.
Response catalogs tool-progress blocks as tool_event elements.
Response does not treat social posts as independently verified facts.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the grok-x-and-web-sources scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.

evals.name: grok-media-and-canvas
evals.prompt: The Grok thread refers to an uploaded Markdown file, generated image and video, and a Canvas object, but only labels are pasted.

expectations.text: Response catalogs the Markdown file, image, video, and Canvas separately.
Response assigns metadata-only or referenced-not-supplied fidelity to absent content.
Response treats Canvas as a separate surface.
Response does not invent visual or file contents.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the grok-media-and-canvas scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.

evals.name: grok-ambiguous-style
evals.prompt: Normalize a flattened Grok conversation with no labels where both people use terse, sarcastic language and one answer quotes a prompt.

expectations.text: Response never assigns roles from tone or style alone.
Response gives alternation-only assignments low confidence.
Response does not automatically split the quoted prompt into a new turn.
Response records unresolved boundaries in Normalization exceptions.

evals.expected_output: A standalone platform-tuned thread-context extract for the grok-ambiguous-style scenario that satisfies all four evidence-anchored expectations.
